name: context-menu
title: Context Menu
description: A menu of application actions revealed by right-clicking or long-pressing an anchor region, or by the keyboard Menu key / Shift+F10. Built on Base UI's Context Menu and sharing the Dropdown Menu's popup and item styling: role=menu with roving focus, first-letter typeahead, checkbox and radio items, grouped labels, destructive items, and nested submenus, cursor-anchored and collision-aware with logical sides that flip in RTL and 44px item targets. It hides functionality behind a gesture, so it must only duplicate actions that are also reachable another way — never be the sole path to an action.
useWhen: A power-user accelerator that duplicates actions already available on visible controls (a right-click shortcut for a row's edit/duplicate/delete that also live in a visible More menu)
Offering the platform-conventional right-click menu on an object as a faster path to actions offered elsewhere
Adding contextual keyboard shortcuts (Menu key / Shift+F10) to actions that a visible button or toolbar already exposes
avoidWhen: The primary or sole access to an action — a context menu is unreachable for most touch users and easy to miss for keyboard users (use a visible Button, toolbar, or Dropdown Menu)
Touch-first flows, where right-click has no equivalent and long-press is undiscoverable
Primary site navigation between pages (use a nav landmark with links)
A long, filterable set of choices bound to a form value (use the Combo Box)

keyboard: Right click or long press on the trigger region opens the menu at the pointer

The Menu key or Shift+F10 opens the menu when a focusable element inside the trigger region has focus (the keyboard contextmenu event bubbles to the trigger), so the region must contain a real focusable control
On open, focus moves into the menu and lands on the first item
Up Arrow and Down Arrow rove between items (roving focus); the list loops at the ends by default
Home jumps to the first item; End jumps to the last item
Typing a character moves the highlight to the next item beginning with it (first-letter typeahead)
Enter or Space activates the highlighted item and closes the menu (actions close by default; checkbox and radio items stay open)
Escape closes the menu and returns focus to the trigger region
Right Arrow opens a submenu and focuses its first item; Left Arrow closes it back to the submenu trigger (mirrored in RTL)
Disabled items stay reachable by the arrow keys (the APG focusable-disabled pattern) but carry aria-disabled and cannot be activated
Checkbox items expose aria-checked (menuitemcheckbox) and radio items expose aria-checked (menuitemradio)
Every action in the menu is also reachable through a visible control, so hiding it behind the gesture never removes access
